13 - How To Avoid Sending Proposals.mp4
So you're going to get the question a lot. Send me a proposal. And you're gonna wonder what you should do in that exact situation. What I've always done in my agency, instead of taking an additional step to get to the agreement where somebody can sign and execute and we can start working together, I incorporate my proposal into my agreement. And I leveraged this process called an SOW, which stands for strategy of work. So one time I'm doing a deal with this guy. His name's Eric Casaburian. He runs this $150 million a year. gym franchise called Retro Fitness. And we help pump some content on Eric's personal page. And we attract people who end up wanting to do some franchise deals with him. We got nine leads for him.
All together, with our engagement campaign process, and then running lead forms towards those people. Now, Eric, right before I was doing a deal with him, because it was going to go into his corporate CFO's approval inbox. He wanted to make sure that I understood what was called an SOW. So he put me on game to this initially. So this comes from what a large level corporation would expect is my point being. You're going to be able to use this down to the small business owners to protect yourself. So an SOW, once again, just stands for a statement of work, a strategy of work, whatever you want to call it. And I'm going to give you an example. So my team and I, we were pitching Jordan Belfort, the Wolf of Wall Street, and him and his son, Bowen, they had asked for a proposal.
And I said, 'Hey, no problem, guys.' And they had mentioned to send one over for consulting and to send one over for consulting and being hands-on as well. So that's exactly what we did, except instead of just sending a proposal, which is really just an itemized set of actions that you're going to be taking when and what to expect. We just simply incorporate that into a strategy of work right inside of our agreement. So these are the exact legal agreements that you guys can download. You get 15 different legal agreements, and I believe there's more than 15 actually. So this is an example of... a consulting deal in addition to being hands-on. So it's for 15K recurring. We split the costs, 7,500 and 7,500, and we explain the terms.
It's just a flat out standard legal agreement that we use for our agency. We then make sure that the client has the ability to sign. We send this through DocuSign, of course. And here's the part that would be a proposal. That most people think they need to separate from the agreement, which really just increases and adds steps to your overall sales cycle. So check this out. A description of services to be rendered. After a payment for terms are as follows. And right here, this is what a strategy of work is. These specific bold statements, purpose, scope of work. And as you can see, this is exactly what we were doing for Jordan and what we pitched him on.
And we made sure that we documented it step-by-step where it takes place, how long it is, what the deliverable schedules are and what is going to be delivered. What the applicable standards are, what the acceptance criteria is, what the special requirements are, types of payment and the payment schedule, miscellaneous payment for info, I mean the payment info section. This is an SOW right here. This is also a proposal. And you, if you would send a proposal instead of a proposal and an agreement, would unfortunately get stuck with adding an additional step to your sales process. So just go ahead and download the legal agreements. Of course, consult with your local legal team and your attorney. Make sure that you guys make this specific to your business, the regulations, wherever you're operating.
At, of course, and make sure you're legal and in good standing. When you leverage this agreement and this type of agreement, it reduces the friction in the process, gets the deal done faster. Course you're sending it right through DocuSign with a payment authorization form at the bottom. So if they like what they see, they can act on it right away. As I've described, it reduces a step in the sales process, getting you closer to the deal faster. Hope this helps you and your agency. I'll see you in the next video.
